claimText: The Long-Period Average (LPA) for rainfall is calculated over a period of 30 to 50 years.
evidenceExcerpt: LPA is calculated over a long period, usually 30-50 years
examUse: Statement-based question on the definition and time interval of the Long-Period Average.

claimText: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) currently uses rainfall data from the period 1971 to 2020 to calculate the Long-Period Average (LPA).
evidenceExcerpt: with the IMD currently using rainfall data from 1971 to 2020.
examUse: Factual statement in a multi-statement question regarding IMD's current baseline for LPA.

claimText: According to the IMD, 'Normal' rainfall is categorized as 96% to 104% of the Long-Period Average (LPA).
evidenceExcerpt: Normal rainfall is categorized as 96% to 104% of the LPA.
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question matching rainfall categories to their LPA percentage ranges.

claimText: According to the IMD, 'Deficient' rainfall is defined as less than 90% of the Long-Period Average (LPA) and indicates drought-like conditions.
evidenceExcerpt: Deficient rainfall, indicating drought-like conditions, is defined as less than 90% of the LPA.
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question matching rainfall categories to their LPA percentage ranges.

claimText: According to the IMD, 'Above Normal' rainfall is categorized as 105% to 110% of the Long-Period Average (LPA).
evidenceExcerpt: Above Normal rainfall is 105% to 110% of the LPA.
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question matching rainfall categories to their LPA percentage ranges.
